series_description: Documentation of the location, extent and characteristics of National Highway System (NHS) Intermodal Connectors (are a subset of the NHS and are public roads leading to major intermodal terminals [airport, port facility, Amtrak station, rail/truck terminal intercity bus terminal, public transit or multi-modal passenger terminal, pipeline terminal or ferry terminal]. The NHS connectors link the highway backbone of the NHS to other modes of transport at their terminal, creating a national intermodal freight system and enabling more efficient use of all freight modes.)and Strategic Highway Corridor Network (STRAHNET) Highways (STRAHNET includes highways which are important to the United States strategic defense policy and which provide defense access, continutity and emergency capabilities for the movement of personnel, materials and equipment in both peacetime and war time.) The National Highway System (NHS) is a network of nationally significant highways approved by Congress in the National Highway System Desgination Act of 1995. It includes the Interstate System and nearly 114,000 miles of arterial and other roads and connectors to major intermodal terminals.
retention_statement: Paper: Permanent

series_description: Electronic data/information files which summarize the findings of individual Origin & Destination surveys. These are extensive field data collections and office analysis efforts to measure travel patterns in and around a defined area. Several days are usually spent stopping and interviewing drivers at key locations around an area of interest to determine where drivers started their trip (origin), where they are going (destination) and the generalizedpurpose of the travel. There are variations of information gathered as well as the methods used to collect it, depending on the questions at hand, but usually the underlying goal is to uptain a sample of trip origins and destinations from drivers in order to forecast the magnitude of diversion of traffic, due to new highways, added or reduced capacity, added or eliminated access, change in tolls, etc. The information collected is usually used to build and/or calibrate travel models, which are used to estimate impacts of various policies and investment strategies on the transportation system.
retention_statement: Electronic: 20 years, or until superceded by a newer O&D study of the same geographic area.

series_description: This is a series of audit files containing audits, reviews, and attestation engagements of consultant, railroad, utility, transit, Local Political Authority (LPA), Metropolitan Planning Organization (MPO), constructions and research claims paid from State funds, with or without Federal reimbursement requested, Confirmations, or other special external audits. Files may contain: Audit, review or attestation engagement reports, audit certification and exceptions, audit index forms, desk audit review forms, workpapers, submitted financial statements and over head schedules, copies of principals, earnings (including W-2 disclosure), accounting system questionnaire responses, all correspondence pertaining to project and/or other miscellaneous documentation.

(Obsoleteness defined as fifth year following final payment or last submitted financial statement.). ; Electronic: Reports: Retain 2 most recently completed audit, review or attestation reports until superceded or five years from report date, whichever period is shorter All Other Documentation: Retain all other documentation until superceded, obsolete or replaced. (Obsoleteness defined as fifth year following final payment or last submitted financial statement.).

series_description: This series consists of all purchasing related documents--agreements (e.g. lease or personal services), supporting documentation, purchase orders, purchase order modifications, payment card user program files, payment card reconciliations, training documents, prior fiscal year extension requests, purchasing guidance documents, release and permit requests, requests for quotation documentation, requisition requests, single/sole source justifications, Voyager fuel logs, Offshore Waiver Request Files and affidavits and correspondence related to the files.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for two years after close of fiscal year. ; Electronic: Retain for two years after close of fiscal year..

series_description: This series consists of documents sent to DAS HRD Payroll identifying individuals ODOT has hired or contracted with (i.e. not ODOT staff). This is used to identify additional work performed by state or non-state workers for work that is not applicable towards OPERS retirement.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for two years after close of fiscal year. ; Electronic: Retain for two years after close of fiscal year.

series_description: This series includes transactional forms used for customer credit card payments either online or in person, made to the State of Ohio to cover damages, training and/or a variety of other receivables.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain through current fiscal year

series_description: This series consists of a complete record of an unpaid accounts receivable invoice that has been certified as a delinquent claim to the Attorney General. Includes invoices and correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper until paid/cancelled. Scan after 2 years and destroy. ; Electronic: Maintain permanently.

series_description: This series contains all reports and revenue documents for the deposit to the State Treasurer showing the receipt source or debtor, quantity and dollar amount being paid as well as the fund to be credited.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three years after close of fiscal year. ; Electronic: Retain for three years after close of fiscal year.

series_description: This series consists of all documentation related to loans and bonds through the State Infrastructure Bank (i.e. application, committee approval, signed term sheet, payment schedule, loan agreement, promissory note, guarantor agreement, draw requests, amendments, Attorney General requested documents, or bond trustee documents).
retention_statement: Paper: Life of Loan / Bond Term + 5 Years. Scan and destroy paper. ; Electronic: Maintain permanently.

series_description: This series consists of miscellaneous correspondence, invoices and worksheets relating to the payment of Motor Vehicle Fuel Tax by the Department of Transportation or the refund of gasoline tax paid by DOT to the Department of Taxation.
retention_statement: Unknown: Retain for one year after close of fiscal year.

series_description: This record series contains Ohio-5501.1-Application for Landing Site. Information consistent with ORC 5501. Applications include Airport/Heliport and Owner information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper forms until scanned electronically ; Electronic: Electronic Form must be kept permanently or until airport is no longer used as such.

series_description: This record series contains Airport/Heliport Biannual Registration consistent with ORC 5501. Registration includes Airport/Heliport and Owner information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain permanently or until airport is no longer used as such. ; Paper: Retain paper forms until scanned electronically

series_description: In the Transportation Systems Management & Operations data warehouse there is a table called dbo.InrixXDdata, which is a historical archive of traffic speeds on Ohio's road network downloaded from ODOT's speed data provider, INRIX. Data in this table is archived for INRIX's XD segment network, and has a resolution of five minute intervals.
retention_statement: Electronic: Current year plus three additional years

series_description: Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR) data collected on state roadway network and assets. The data is collected with the pavement condition data on the state roadway network. Interstates are collected annually. US and State routes are collected on a two-year cycle, half of the state each year.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain permanently

series_description: Correspondence and casework received and maintained by the ODOT Contact Center.
retention_statement: Paper: Five years ; Electronic: Five years

series_description: Recordings of Contact Center calls. The record is saved for 45 consecutive days and deleted on the 46th day.
retention_statement: Electronic: 45 days

series_description: Recordings of Contact Center calls obtained from the server.
retention_statement: Electronic: 5 years

series_title: ADA Accommodation Requests under Title II (Participants in ODOT programs, activities, services)
series_description: This series consists of records maintained by ODOT for ADA accommodation requests under Title II made by participants in ODOT programs, activities, or services.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper until scanned electronically ; Electronic: Retain electronic records for seven (7) years from the date a determination for accommodation is made
